messages.content:
I am writing an email to the Norwegian directorate of health,

i am addressing my grievences with them and providing them with some additioanl information they asked for,

Subject: Concerns and Additional Information Regarding Public Health Issues

Dear Director of the Norwegian Directorate of Health,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to address some concerns I have regarding public health issues in Norway, and to provide you with additional information that you had requested.

Firstly, I would like to express my concerns about the lack of accessibility to mental health services in certain regions of the country. It has come to my attention that some individuals are struggling to access the necessary support and resources for their mental well-being, which is a growing concern in today's society. I urge the Directorate of Health to take a proactive approach in addressing this issue and ensuring that everyone has access to the mental health services they require.

Secondly, I would like to bring to your attention the importance of promoting healthy lifestyle choices and preventive measures to combat the rising rates of chronic diseases in Norway. As a nation, it is crucial that we prioritize preventive healthcare initiatives and support individuals in making informed decisions about their health and well-being.

Lastly, I have enclosed some additional information that you had requested regarding recent public health surveys and data analysis. I believe that this information will provide valuable insights into the current state of public health in Norway and help inform future policy decisions.

I appreciate your attention to these matters and look forward to discussing potential solutions and initiatives to improve public health in Norway. Thank you for your time and consideration.

Warm regards,

[Your Name]

This is the email i am sending, please review it and give your thoughts, 

Dear Monica,


Allow me to explain why I have not responded to your previous email,


I began my application more than 2 years ago, after i had inquired whether it would be considered from the EEA, I was told by yourselves via email, that such information was available on your website. Indeed it was, Cyprus, where my university(Eastern Mediterranean University) is located, was indeed on your list of EEA countries.


Following your own advise, basing the lodging of my application on the information available on your website, i lodged it as an EEA application.


My case officer at the time whose name shall not be mentioned as i do not know if the information was confidential or not, requested several documents not listed on the required documents or even possible documents list without any explanation as to why they would be required, then proceeded to completely ignore my emails and inquiries, not once responding to any of them. Still, i sent whatever documents she had additionally requested to her as well as your main address while informing yourselves that she was not responsive.


fast forward to 6/22/2022, around 6 months later, I saw there was a form on altinn, claiming that additional documents were needed as my application had not been from the EEA. with no substantiation as to how yourselves had arrived at such a conclusion, contradicting your very own information page, which still to this date, lists Cyprus as an EEA country.


I am in shock that a country that claims to be in dire need our profession, would discard us so carelessly.


In the time it took yourselves to reach a conclusion that I had inquired about since even before the lodging of my application, an inquiry that should have been addressed at the beginning, I obtained a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and have since lodged a Visa application with Australia. Still, I will not abandon my application with you, seeing as to how you have inquired about it long after the deadline you had previously set. This, I am grateful to see.


The documents you have asked for are attached to this email, except the previous work document, which i do not possess as i have not been employed, I've also attached my Australian qualification assessment just in case it helps the process,


If it is not too much to ask, I at least hope for an explanation as to why my application was handled in a manner, I consider to be unbecoming of the Helsedirektoratet. I also ask that you would consider reviewing my application as an EEA application, which I believe it should be considered as, in accordance with your own website.


Thank you for your time and understanding,


Sincerely
Ali Khosravi

Overall, your email effectively conveys your frustration and concerns with the handling of your application by the Norwegian Directorate of Health. However, there are a few suggestions for improvement:

1. Tone: While it is important to express your disappointment, try to maintain a professional and respectful tone throughout the email.

2. Clarity: Ensure that your message is clear and to the point. Consider breaking down the information into more digestible paragraphs for better readability.

3. Proofreading: Check for any grammatical or spelling errors to enhance the professionalism of your email.

4. Providing Evidence: Back up your claims with specific examples and evidence to strengthen your argument and support your request for a review of your application as an EEA application.

5. Conclusion: End the email with a polite and courteous tone, expressing gratitude for their consideration and understanding.

Overall, your email effectively communicates your grievances and requests for clarification and review. With a few adjustments in tone and clarity, your message will be more impactful in addressing your concerns to the Norwegian Directorate of Health.
don't give me general ideas and basic things that i've already done, give me speecifics, you think tone is bad somewhere, tell me where. you tthink evidence is lacking somewhere? show me where

Dear Monica,

I am writing in regards to the handling of my application for consideration as an EEA applicant. I want to address the discrepancies in the information provided by the Norwegian Directorate of Health and the lack of communication from my assigned case officer.

When beginning my application over two years ago, I followed the advice given by your team via email and the information available on your website, which listed Cyprus as an EEA country. Based on this, I submitted my application as an EEA applicant. However, my case officer at the time requested additional documents without explanation and failed to respond to any of my inquiries. Despite my efforts to comply with her requests and inform your office of her unresponsiveness, the application process became unnecessarily challenging.

Recently, on 6/22/2022, I was surprised to find a form on Altinn stating that my application was not considered from the EEA, contradicting your information page listing Cyprus as an EEA country. This discrepancy has caused confusion and delays in the processing of my application. Additionally, without any substantiation or explanation from your team, I am left questioning the accuracy and consistency of the information provided.

In the time it took for your team to reach this conclusion, I pursued a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and initiated a visa application to Australia. Although I am grateful for your recent inquiry about my application, I am disappointed with the handling of my case and the lack of timely communication.

I have attached the requested documents to this email, with the exception of a previous work document as I have not been employed. Additionally, I have included my Australian qualification assessment for your reference.

I kindly request an explanation for the handling of my application and urge you to reconsider its classification as an EEA application, as per the information on your website.

Thank you for your time and understanding. I look forward to resolving this matter promptly.

Sincerely, <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Indeed it was, Cyprus, where my university(Eastern Mediterranean University) is located, was indeed on your list of EEA countries.

Over two years ago, I inquired about the eligibility of my application as an EEA applicant. I was informed via email that the relevant information could be found on your website, where Cyprus, the location of my university (Eastern Mediterranean University), was listed as an EEA country.

I lodged my application as an EEA applicant based on the information provided on your website, following your advice.

Still, i sent whatever documents she had additionally requested to her as well as your main address while informing yourselves that she was not responsive.
My previous case officer requested undisclosed documents without explanation and failed to respond to my inquiries, prompting me to send the requested documents to both her and the main office.

rewrite this more concise: fast forward to 6/22/2022, around 6 months later, I saw there was a form on altinn, claiming that additional documents were needed as my application had not been from the EEA. with no substantiation as to how yourselves had arrived at such a conclusion, contradicting your very own information page, which still to this date, lists Cyprus as an EEA country.

On 6/22/2022, I discovered a form on Altinn requesting additional documents due to my application not being considered from the EEA. This contradicted your website's listing of Cyprus as an EEA country with no explanation provided.

rewrite this more concise: In the time it took yourselves to reach a conclusion that I had inquired about since even before the lodging of my application, an inquiry that should have been addressed at the beginning, I obtained a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and have since lodged a Visa application with Australia. Still, I will not abandon my application with you, seeing as to how you have inquired about it long after the deadline you had previously set. This, I am grateful to see.

While waiting for your conclusion on my initial inquiry, I obtained a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and applied for a visa to Australia. I intend to maintain my application with you, despite the delay in your response to my inquiry, for which I am grateful.
a more respectful word for long overdue?
Belated.

rewrite this better, longer or shorter doesn't matter, justt better : While waiting for your belated conclusion on my initial inquiry, I obtained a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and applied for a visa to Australia. However, I intend to maintain my application with you out of renewed respect for you, since you extended the deadline of your own accord.

While awaiting your delayed response to my initial inquiry, I proceeded to obtain a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and submitted a visa application for Australia. Despite these developments, I have chosen to uphold my application with your organization as a gesture of appreciation for your gracious extension of the application deadline.
rewrite this more concise

While waiting for your delayed response to my inquiry, I pursued a qualification assessment from the Australian Pharmacy Council and applied for a visa to Australia. Nevertheless, I will maintain my application with you in gratitude for the extended deadline.

rewrite this more consie : I will not ask for an explanation as to why my application was handled in such a way as I know there may be things beyond your control, but I urge you to reconsider its classification as an EEA application, as per the information on your website.

I won't seek an explanation for the handling of my application, understanding factors beyond your control. Yet, I kindly request a review of its classification as an EEA application, in line with the information on your website.
